title: Fix 401 on /api/usage/ingest: verify OAuth JWT against issuer, not bare origin
description: ClaudeMeter OAuth tokens get 401 at /api/usage/ingest. Root cause: validateMcpAuth verifies audience=BETTER_AUTH_URL (bare origin https://ruchern.dev) but the better-auth provider issues JWTs with aud=issuer=https://ruchern.dev/api/auth (baseURL + /api/auth basePath). verifyAccessToken throws, silent catch swallows it, falls through to 401. Fix: add OAUTH_RESOURCE=${BASE_URL}/api/auth single source of truth; verify audience+issuer against it; fix protected-resource metadata; log JWT-shaped verification failures.

title: Promote [data-minimal] CSS tokens to :root and swap (main) layout shell
description:
The minimal preview validated a white/black B&W aesthetic. This is the foundational CSS + layout change that all page redesign issues depend on.

## What changes

### globals.css
- Merge the [data-minimal] token block into the :root light theme, replacing coral values:
  - --accent: oklch(0.145 0.003 248) (was oklch(0.6 0.18 25) coral)
  - --accent-foreground: oklch(1 0 0) (white)
  - --background: oklch(1 0 0) (pure white, was off-white #f7f5f3)
  - --surface: oklch(0.985 0 0)
  - --muted: oklch(0.52 0.005 250)
  - --default: oklch(0.96 0 0)
  - --border: oklch(0.91 0.002 250)
- Remove the [data-minimal] scoped block (it is now the default)
- Keep [data-apple] block as-is (used by preview)
- Update dark mode if any coral references sneak in there

### (main)/layout.tsx
- Replace `<Header />` with `<MinimalHeader />` (or merge them — see notes)
- Replace `<Footer />` with `<MinimalFooter />`
- Remove `<BackgroundEffects />` entirely
- Remove the `data-minimal` attribute (no longer needed once :root is updated)

### manifest.ts
- background_color: '#ffffff'
- theme_color: '#1a1a1a' (tracked separately in blog-tko but can land here)

## Notes
- MinimalHeader and MinimalFooter live in apps/web/src/app/(minimal)/components/. Move them to apps/web/src/components/ (or apps/web/src/app/(main)/components/) and rename to Header/Footer before deleting (minimal).
- After this lands, all (main) pages will have the white/black palette but still their old widget-heavy layouts. The page-level redesign issues pick up from here.
- The coral accent is also used in BackgroundEffects gradient colours — removing BackgroundEffects is sufficient, no need to hunt for coral hex values elsewhere.
acceptance_criteria: globals.css has no [data-minimal] block; :root uses eclipse accent; (main)/layout.tsx has no BackgroundEffects; MinimalHeader and MinimalFooter (or equivalent) are in place; pnpm typecheck passes; pnpm build passes

title: Phase 4: Publish via Changesets, remove semantic-release
description: Add .changeset/config.json (access public). Remove semantic-release deps, .releaserc.json, release script; swap release workflow to Changesets action. Verify pnpm publish -r --dry-run = one self-contained @ruchern/blog-mcp tarball with @repo/* inlined. Cut 0.1.0.

title: Phase 3: Extract @ruchern/blog-mcp publishable package
description: Finish turning the existing packages/mcp workspace package into a publishable @ruchern/blog-mcp package. Add package bin/exports/publishConfig/build output, decide how web-owned dependencies are packaged or injected, keep the Next.js /api/mcp route and local stdio command working, and verify the published tarball is self-contained enough for intended consumers.
notes: Deferred. packages/mcp already exists and apps/web consumes @workspace/mcp/server. Remaining work is publishability, build output, bin wiring, and dependency boundaries; not the initial move from src/mcp.

title: Phase 2: Extract @repo/core + decouple next/cache
description: Move shared subset MCP needs (post-metadata, services: media/r2/popular-posts/cache-invalidation, config: cache.config/redis, pure getPostsWithOverlappingTags, needed utils/constants) to packages/core. Add cache-port (injectable revalidate) + web setRevalidate adapter. Keep 'use cache' reads in app. Verify grep next/ in packages/core/src empty + tests green.
close_reason: Dropped: @repo/core only existed to host code MCP shares; with MCP staying in apps/web there is nothing to extract. next/cache decoupling deferred until an actual MCP extraction.

title: Phase 1: Extract @repo/db package
description: Move src/schema/*, migrations/, drizzle.config.ts to packages/db. Refactor eager neon singleton into createDb(connStr) factory + lazy db Proxy default. Rewrite @/schema -> @repo/db (~29 files). Point db:* scripts + vercel-build at --filter @repo/db. Verify db:generate no diff + migrate dry-run.
close_reason: Extracted @repo/db (schema/, drizzle.ts client, barrel index, migrations, drizzle.config). Rewrote 30 @/schema imports to @repo/db; db:* scripts moved to the package, root delegates --filter @repo/db, apps/web vercel-build migrates via @repo/db. Verified: typecheck (both), no schema drift, tests, build.

title: Phase 0: Scaffold pnpm + Turborepo workspace
description: Install turbo; add turbo.json (build/typecheck/test/lint/dev/db tasks), tsconfig.base.json, .changeset/. Set pnpm-workspace.yaml to ['.', 'packages/*'] preserving allowBuilds + savePrefix. Convert root scripts to turbo run delegators. No code moves. Verify turbo run build test typecheck green and FULL TURBO on second run.
close_reason: Workspace scaffolded: turbo 2.9.16 + pnpm workspace (apps/*, packages/*); app relocated to apps/web (@repo/web); root slimmed to turbo delegators + shared tooling. Verified: typecheck/lint/test/build green, build+typecheck hit FULL TURBO on 2nd run.

Preserve coral OKLCH look, migrate recharts to HeroUI Pro charts, delete shadcn at end. Incremental by phase. Plan: ~/.claude/plans/migrate-shadcn-heroui-twinkly-penguin.md

This work should document how HeroUI Pro coexists with shadcn/ui while shadcn remains legacy during migration.

The document should use name: Blog, describe HeroUI Pro Default as the target design system, and capture the warm editorial colour direction, typography, layout, components, and do/don't rules for the blog redesign.

description: Convert every raw-HTML component with a HeroUI equivalent to HeroUI (Pro first, then OSS), re-skinned to notebook aesthetic. Keep the 4 bespoke signature primitives. Header rebuilt on Pro Navbar. See plan.

Tables wired to HeroUI built-in selection; bespoke cell padding/hover now use HeroUI Table defaults (intentional). Not yet done: manual browser verification (dev server).

title: Address PR #324 review comments (OAuth provider)
description: Implement the 4 human review comments + actionable Codex comments on PR #324: named function in consent-form, HeroUI isPending button state, split auth error IDs, tighten turbo.json, require mcp scope, reject disabled clients, protected-resource metadata route, align Portless docs, preserve OAuth flow through login. Plus PR replies on the 2 already-resolved Codex P1s.
notes: Implemented: named ConsentForm fn; HeroUI isPending+Spinner on consent/login buttons; split auth IDs into auth-error-ids.ts (AUTH_ERROR); mcp scope in oauthProvider + route-level 403 insufficient_scope gate; disabled-client rejection in validateMcpAuth; RFC9728 /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource route + WWW-Authenticate; blog.localhost in .env.example; preserve OAuth flow through login; static token marked for deletion (kept). Docs updated. Turbo.json left as-is per decision. Gates: typecheck/lint/tests green (98 passed).

title: Migrate OAuth provider from deprecated oidcProvider to @better-auth/oauth-provider
description: oidcProvider plugin is deprecated and removed in Better Auth 1.7. Migrate auth.ts, schema/oauth.ts (oauthClient/oauthRefreshToken/oauthAccessToken/oauthConsent), mcp-auth.ts token lookup, add required consent page, update docs. No prod data to preserve (feature not yet shipped) so clean schema swap via db:push.

title: Add OAuth provider
description: Make the blog an OAuth 2.1 / OIDC provider (Better Auth oidcProvider plugin) so clients can authenticate users via the Authorization Code flow with PKCE instead of the static BLOG_MCP_AUTH_TOKEN. Add oauth provider tables, enable dynamic client registration for public clients, and validate OAuth access tokens at protected routes. Keep the static token path for headless MCP/CLI clients during migration.

title: Add time-range (X days) filter to Usage page

description: The /usage Breakdown card only visualised tokens; cost was hidden in a tooltip. Replace the bar chart with a sortable HeroUI Pro DataGrid showing tokens, cost, blended $/1M tokens, messages, and a 90-day token trend sparkline per model/provider/agent.

title: Redesign main projects page to feature-row and compact archive grid
description:
Replace the current projects page (PageTitle + Widget-based featured cards + ItemCard archive) with a split feature-row layout for featured projects and a compact 3-column archive grid. Reference: apps/web/src/app/(minimal)/minimal/projects/page.tsx for the approved minimal design.

Current: apps/web/src/app/(main)/projects/page.tsx uses PageTitle, Widget components from HeroUI Pro for featured projects (with cover images), and ItemCard for the archive section.

Target layout:

Featured projects (one per row, alternating or consistent split):
  Each project = grid md:grid-cols-2 rounded-2xl overflow-hidden border border-border
  Left panel: bg-foreground p-12 flex items-end — project name in large light type (font-bold text-4xl text-background/20 or large)
  Right panel: p-8 flex flex-col gap-4 bg-background — name (again, readable), description, skill tags, live/GitHub links

Archive (non-featured projects):
  3-column minimal grid: grid grid-cols-1 gap-4 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3
  Each card: rounded-2xl border border-border p-5 flex flex-col gap-2
  Project name as link + 2-line description clamped + tech stack as small dot-separated text

Link handling:
  Live URL = first link in project.links that is NOT github.com
  GitHub URL = first link in project.links that IS github.com
  Use isGitHubUrl() helper (already in resume/page.tsx — extract to lib/utils or replicate inline)

Components to DELETE after this (check for other usages first):
  No (main)/projects-specific components exist — projects page uses shared Widget from HeroUI Pro. Removing Widget usage here is sufficient.

Data: projects data file (apps/web/src/data/projects.ts) — unchanged. Featured projects = projects.filter(p => p.featured), archive = the rest.

Depends on blog-8d0 being merged first.
acceptance_criteria: /projects shows split-panel feature rows for featured projects; compact archive grid below; no Widget/PageTitle; live and GitHub links render correctly; pnpm typecheck passes

title: Redesign main blog list page to editorial magazine layout
description:
Replace the current blog list page (FeaturedPost + SeriesCards + PostGrid) with an editorial magazine layout: a full dark featured post card, then a card grid for all posts. Reference: apps/web/src/app/(minimal)/minimal/blog/page.tsx for the approved minimal design.

Current: apps/web/src/app/(main)/blog/page.tsx imports FeaturedPost (apps/web/src/app/(main)/blog/components/featured-post.tsx), SeriesCards, PostGrid from the (main) blog components directory.

Target layout:
1. Page heading row: H1 'Writing.' + subtitle, left-aligned
2. Featured post card: rounded-2xl overflow-hidden. bg-foreground background, white text overlay. Title in text-2xl font-bold text-background. Summary in text-background/60. Date + tags at bottom-left. Full-width card, not a grid cell.
3. Series section: horizontal scroll pills (keep existing SeriesCards pattern or simplify to plain scrollable links)
4. All posts grid: grid grid-cols-1 gap-4 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3. Each card: date at top in muted small text, title in font-bold, 2-line excerpt clamped, reading time + tag pills at bottom.

Scroll-reveal animation for post cards using motion/react-client:
  initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 20 }}
  whileInView={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
  viewport={{ once: true, amount: 0.2 }}
  transition={{ type: 'spring', stiffness: 400, damping: 30 }}

Post cards on hover: translateY(-2px) with transition-transform.

No plain divide-y list. Cards with genuine visual weight and hover lift.

Components to DELETE after this (check for other usages first):
- apps/web/src/app/(main)/blog/components/featured-post.tsx (replaced by new inline dark card)
- apps/web/src/app/(main)/blog/components/post-grid.tsx (replaced by new card grid)
- apps/web/src/app/(main)/blog/components/series-cards.tsx and series-cards.client.tsx (simplify or replace)

Keep: tag-filter.tsx, tag-selector.tsx (may still be used), series-navigation.tsx (used in blog/[slug]).

Data: getPublishedPosts(), getFeaturedPost(), getSeries() — all stay as-is.

Depends on blog-8d0 being merged first.
acceptance_criteria: /blog shows dark featured post card at top; post grid renders in 1/2/3 columns; scroll-reveal animations on post cards; series section present; no old Widget/PageTitle components; pnpm typecheck passes

title: Redesign main about page to two-column sticky split
description:
Replace the current about page (PageTitle + KPI widgets + Employment) with a two-column layout: sticky identity panel left, scrollable content right. Reference: apps/web/src/app/(minimal)/minimal/about/page.tsx for the approved minimal design.

Current: apps/web/src/app/(main)/about/page.tsx uses PageTitle with icon, KPI/KPIGroup widgets from HeroUI Pro, and the Employment component. Heavy widget-based layout.

Target layout:
- Wrapper: grid md:grid-cols-[260px_1fr] gap-12 md:gap-16
- Left (sticky): md:sticky md:top-28 md:self-start. Contains avatar, name, role, location, social links as minimal icon buttons. No HeroUI widgets.
- Right (scrollable): flex flex-col gap-12. Contains: bio paragraph, three-cell mini bento (years / companies / current employer) as grid grid-cols-3 gap-3 each rounded-2xl border p-4, then Employment section.

Stat mini bento cells: each shows a number (8, 4, DBS) in font-bold text-2xl, label in text-muted text-xs uppercase tracking-widest below. No KPI/KPIGroup components.

Employment component (apps/web/src/app/(main)/about/components/employment.tsx) is shared and should be kept. No changes to it.

Components to DELETE after this (no longer used):
- apps/web/src/app/(main)/about/components/about-card.tsx
- apps/web/src/app/(main)/about/components/experience-card.tsx
- apps/web/src/app/(main)/about/components/globe.tsx
- apps/web/src/app/(main)/about/components/location-card.tsx
- apps/web/src/app/(main)/about/components/timeline-thread.tsx
- apps/web/src/app/(main)/about/components/contributions.tsx

Keep: employment.tsx (shared), experience-card if still referenced elsewhere (check first).

Depends on blog-8d0 (layout shell) being merged first.
acceptance_criteria: /about renders two-column on desktop, single column mobile; left sticky panel scrolls independently; stats show as mini 3-cell bento; Employment section renders; deleted components removed; pnpm typecheck passes

title: Redesign main home page to bento grid layout
description:
Replace the current three-section home (HeroSection + FeaturedWork + LatestPosts) with an asymmetric bento grid that IS the hero, no traditional above-the-fold section.

Current structure: apps/web/src/app/(main)/page.tsx imports MinimalHeroSection (post count, stars, visits), FeaturedWork (projects), and LatestPosts. All render as stacked full-width sections.

Target layout on desktop (md:grid-cols-3):
- Bio cell md:col-span-2: h1 + tagline in a rounded-2xl border card
- Avatar/status cell: avatar image + open-to-work status + CTA link
- Two dark stat cells (post count, GitHub stars): bg-foreground text-background rounded-2xl — inverted
- Featured project cell md:col-span-2: project name + description + stack + links
- Latest posts row md:col-span-3: 3 posts as divide-y list inside a single bordered card
Mobile: single column stack of all cells.

Grid: grid grid-cols-1 gap-4 md:grid-cols-3

Animation: import as motion from motion/react-client (RSC-compatible, no use client needed). Stagger cells in with staggerChildren: 0.07, each cell hidden: { opacity: 0, y: 16 } to visible: { opacity: 1, y: 0, transition: { type: spring, stiffness: 400, damping: 30 } }.

Data fetches stay unchanged: getPublishedPostsCount(), getGitHubStars(), getTotalVisits(), featured project from projects data, getLatestPosts(3).

Files to change:
- Rewrite apps/web/src/app/(main)/page.tsx to bento grid
- Replace minimal-hero-section.tsx, minimal-featured-work.tsx, minimal-latest-posts.tsx with inline bento cell components (or delete if inlined)

Depends on blog-8d0 (layout shell + CSS tokens) being merged first.
acceptance_criteria: / renders bento grid on desktop (3 cols), single column on mobile; dark stat cells with inverted colours; motion stagger on load; no gradient orbs; pnpm typecheck passes

title: Deploy MCP package as Cloudflare Worker with Wrangler
description: Add a Wrangler-based Cloudflare Worker deployment path for packages/mcp while keeping the existing local stdio MCP server working. Use dev as local wrangler dev and production as the deployed Worker. Port only relevant Worker-compatible ideas from feature/prepare-mcp-worker; do not bring over Alchemy IaC, root app restructuring, or package deletions.
acceptance_criteria: packages/mcp has a Worker HTTP entrypoint for MCP requests; packages/mcp has Wrangler configuration for local dev and production deploy; package scripts expose Wrangler dev/deploy commands; existing stdio entrypoint remains functional; typecheck and MCP tests pass.
notes:
Implemented initial Wrangler Worker path on feature/mcp-wrangler-worker. Added packages/mcp Worker HTTP entrypoint, shared HTTP handler, Worker-safe cache invalidation services, Wrangler config, dev vars example, scripts, and workerd allowBuilds entry. Verification: pnpm --filter @workspace/mcp typecheck passed; pnpm --filter @workspace/mcp test passed; pnpm --filter @workspace/mcp lint passed with pre-existing noExplicitAny warnings; pnpm --filter @workspace/mcp exec wrangler deploy --dry-run passed; pnpm typecheck passed; git diff --check passed.
Opened PR: https://github.com/ruchernchong/blog/pull/320
Current branch check: PR #320 is closed unmerged, and no Wrangler Worker entrypoint/config exists in packages/mcp. Treat this as unstarted/open unless the Worker deployment path is no longer wanted.

title: Prepare blog MCP for Cloudflare Worker runtime
description: Make the existing blog MCP reusable from a Cloudflare Worker entrypoint without adding SST setup. Keep the Next.js route and local stdio server working, document the future Worker endpoint, and cover Worker-specific auth and filesystem limitations.
close_reason: Implemented Worker-ready MCP runtime without SST setup

title: Price OpenAI fast-mode (priority tier) gpt-5.5 usage
description: models.dev only carries the standard gpt-5.5 rate, so fast-mode (priority tier) usage is under-priced. Add a provider-scoped PRIORITY_RATES override in pricing.ts with OpenAI's published priority rate (input $12.50/M, cached $1.25/M, output $75/M = 2.5x standard) and return it from priceFor before the models.dev lookup. Bake into existing costUsd; no schema/PK/display changes. Per-model always-priority config.
notes: Override keyed openai->gpt-5.5-fast (the actual OpenCode model id for the fast tier), NOT plain gpt-5.5. Plain gpt-5.5 stays on standard models.dev rate. Verified via re-ingest: gpt-5.5 openai = $1029.02 (standard), gpt-5.5-fast openai = $3439.60 (priority 2.5x). N.A. rows dropped 37->13. 7 pricing tests pass; typecheck + lint clean on changed files.
close_reason: Shipped in a8df0fa: PRIORITY_RATES override prices openai/gpt-5.5-fast at the priority tier (2.5x); plain gpt-5.5 stays standard. Verified end-to-end via re-ingest.

description: src/app/api/mcp/__tests__/route.test.ts has 2 failing tests (POST/auth paths) due to the mocked WebStandardStreamableHTTPServerTransport not being constructable. Pre-existing on clean main (confirmed via git stash), unrelated to the usage-ingest work. Likely an MCP SDK export/mock shape drift. Fix the vi.mock factory to return a proper constructor.

title: Add authenticated /api/usage/ingest route for prod usage ingestion
description: The usage:ingest script parses LOCAL agent logs and can only run on the local machine, but local env points at the Neon dev branch. Add a POST /api/usage/ingest route that upserts already-aggregated token_usage rows using the deployment's own DATABASE_URL (prod on Vercel), gated by BLOG_MCP_AUTH_TOKEN / admin session. Local script computes rows and POSTs them, so no prod DB credentials ever touch the laptop. Route revalidates the 'usage' cache tag after writing.
close_reason: Implemented /api/usage/ingest route + script remote path; tests/typecheck/lint pass, committed in 00226ff and pushed

title: Add OpenCode support to /usage token-usage profile
description: Add OpenCode (SQLite opencode.db, multi-provider) as a third agent in the usage profile. Read source via node:sqlite; destination stays Neon Postgres token_usage. Requires widening the token_usage PK to (date, agent, provider, model), adding provider to UsageEvent, a per-event provider resolver, an opencode parser, and a pricing signature change to accept explicit provider. Plan: ~/.claude/plans/i-think-i-need-functional-hamming.md
notes: Implemented: opencode SQLite parser (node:sqlite, no new deps), provider?: on UsageEvent, resolveProvider(), pricing now takes {agent,provider}, ingest folds on (date,agent,provider,model), token_usage PK widened to include provider. 14 new unit tests pass; typecheck clean. db:push applied + usage:ingest run: opencode=2.96B tokens across openai/fireworks-ai/opencode-go/opencode/ollama. Verified in DB. Pending: commit+push.
close_reason: Implemented, tested (14 new unit tests), schema pushed, ingested + verified in DB, committed 8fc78a2 and pushed.

title: Add provider dimension to token-usage (derived from agent)
description: Track inference provider (anthropic/openai) as a separate, queryable dimension alongside agent on the /usage feature. Provider is functionally derived from the agent (claude->anthropic, codex->openai) via a single shared source of truth reused by pricing. Additive: agent column/label unchanged; add provider column+index, store it at ingest, add byProvider rollup + providers summary, render a By provider breakdown card.
close_reason: Added provider dimension (derived from agent) across schema, ingest, queries, types and /usage page. db:push applied; re-ingest backfilled 172 rows (anthropic/claude=42, openai/codex=130), no 'unknown' remaining. Typecheck + Biome clean.

title: Token mix stacked bar on /usage

Data already aggregated per day in token_usage; expose an all-time tokenMix total on UsageProfile and render with HeroUI Pro BarChart. RSC shell + client leaf.

title: Build /usage contribution heatmap (KIV — cell component approach undecided)
description: The GitHub-style daily contribution heatmap is the page centrepiece but is on hold. Open question: how to render ~250 day cells given HeroUI-first directive vs dense custom grid — options were (a) HeroUI Button+Tooltip per active cell, (b) lightweight cells + one hover-driven HeroUI Tooltip, (c) hand-rolled SVG + native title. Pure layout helper src/lib/usage/heatmap-layout.ts is already built and ready. DayContribution has per-day totals + intensity (0-4 quantile) + tokenBreakdown + per-agent breakdown for the tooltip. Cost may be null (render N.A.).

title: Price Codex outlier models via MODEL_ALIASES (codex-auto-review, legacy unknown)
description: codex-auto-review (~27.6M tokens) and legacy v0.34.0 'unknown' (~18.7M tokens) are absent from models.dev/LiteLLM/OpenRouter. Add an agent-scoped MODEL_ALIASES map in src/lib/usage/pricing.ts resolving codex:{codex-auto-review->gpt-5-codex, unknown->gpt-5} for cost only (identical $1.25/$10/$0.125 tier). Parser stays faithful; labels unchanged. Then db:push + usage:ingest to verify non-zero cost.

title: Remove (preview)/preview/ route group
description:
The (preview) route group at apps/web/src/app/(preview)/ was a design staging area for an Apple-inspired aesthetic. It has been superseded. This issue removes it entirely.

Files to delete (all of apps/web/src/app/(preview)/):
- (preview)/layout.tsx
- (preview)/preview/page.tsx

Also check and remove:
- Any navigation links to /preview/* in the header, footer, or sitemap
- Any internal links pointing to /preview/* in other pages
- The [data-apple] CSS block in apps/web/src/app/globals.css (and its dark variant [data-apple].dark) — remove the entire scoped block
- Any AppleHeader/AppleFooter imports anywhere outside (preview)/

Grep targets before deleting:
  grep -r '/preview' apps/web/src --include='*.tsx' --include='*.ts'
  grep -r 'data-apple' apps/web/src --include='*.css' --include='*.tsx'
  grep -r 'apple-header' apps/web/src
  grep -r 'AppleHeader' apps/web/src

This is independent — can be done before or after the (main) redesign issues.

title: Refine main blog post page to minimal focused reading mode
description:
The blog post page (apps/web/src/app/(main)/blog/[slug]/page.tsx) already shares most components with the minimal variant and is close to the target. Only minor refinements are needed, no structural rewrite.

Current: Uses ScrollProgress, StatsBar, MDX renderer, TOC sidebar, RelatedPosts. Centred single-column prose with sidebar TOC on desktop. This layout is already clean and reading-focused.

Changes needed:
1. Title display: Increase to text-4xl md:text-5xl for more display weight. Currently uses a custom Typography component or h1 — check and bump the size class.
2. Summary/description callout: Replace the current box-style callout with a left-border accent treatment: border-l-4 border-foreground pl-4 italic text-muted (or similar). More editorial, less card-like.
3. Remove any coral accent references in the post page itself (e.g. tag chip colours, link colours) — these should resolve from the global token change in blog-8d0.
4. RelatedPosts: Verify it looks correct after the token change (no coral accent badges or borders remaining).

Components to audit:
- apps/web/src/app/(main)/blog/components/mdx.tsx — check for hardcoded coral colours
- apps/web/src/app/(main)/blog/components/stats-bar.tsx — check accent usage
- apps/web/src/app/(main)/blog/components/related-posts.tsx — check chip/badge colours
- apps/web/src/app/(main)/blog/components/series-navigation.tsx — check accent usage

No new components needed. This is a refinement pass, not a rewrite.

Depends on blog-8d0 being merged first (CSS token changes resolve most colour issues automatically).
acceptance_criteria: Blog post title is larger (4xl/5xl); summary uses left-border treatment not a box; no coral colour visible anywhere on the post page; RelatedPosts renders correctly; pnpm typecheck passes

description:
Replace the coral circle favicon/PWA icon with a near-black circle. Changes: background #d4513b → #1a1a1a, fontWeight 600 → 800, remove -15deg tilt. Update manifest theme_color and background_color to match. Must work at 16px favicon and 512px PWA splash sizes.

Files: apps/web/src/lib/og/icon.tsx, apps/web/src/app/manifest.ts

Design: Black circle (#1a1a1a), white R, weight 800, upright (no rotation). Circle shape retained for favicon legibility at 16px.

title: Future: hybrid usage ingestion via Vercel Workflow
description: KIV. Optional enhancement to usage ingestion: local CLI parses ~/.claude + ~/.codex (must stay local), then POSTs daily aggregates to an authed API route that start()s a Vercel Workflow to durably price -> upsert -> revalidate the /usage cache. Adds retries/observability on the cloud half. Parsing cannot move to the cloud (Workflow steps run on Vercel, no local fs). Blocked-by/after the local CLI ingestion (blog-4yt).

title: Integrate WakaTime AI activity stats
description:
## Context

WakaTime has an AI Overview dashboard showing AI-related coding metrics such as AI-attributed lines, AI vs human changes, prompt counts, token usage, and follow-up rates. We want to KIV integrating a documented subset of these stats into the site dashboard.

## Scope

Investigate and implement WakaTime AI activity stats using documented APIs only. The documented WakaTime API appears to expose AI activity indirectly through heartbeats at GET /api/v1/users/current/heartbeats?date=YYYY-MM-DD. Heartbeats may include category: "ai coding", line_additions, and line_deletions.

## Supported Metrics

This can support AI-attributed lines, human-attributed lines, AI additions/deletions, human additions/deletions, and an AI vs Human by day chart.

## Out Of Scope

Input tokens, output tokens, AI prompts, average prompt length, human follow-up rate, and human follow-up edits are excluded unless WakaTime exposes an official documented API for them.

## Implementation Direction

Add a server-only WakaTime client in src/lib/wakatime.ts. Add WAKATIME_API_KEY to environment configuration. Aggregate heartbeats over a configurable date range. Display summary cards in /dashboard. Optionally add a Recharts bar chart for AI vs Human by day. Avoid undocumented dashboard or private endpoints.
acceptance_criteria:
WakaTime API key is never exposed client-side.
Missing API key fails gracefully.
Dashboard can show AI vs human line-change stats when data is available.
Token and prompt metrics are excluded unless WakaTime exposes an official API.
Beads issue is linked or synced with the GitHub issue.
